youtube_items.title: [YT] What is Solar Wind and how affect the Earth - YouTube
youtube_items.summary: The space in which the planet's orbit seems to be empty. However, it's not. It's full of matter from the sun. The sun generates powerful magnetic fields that rise above the surface in giant loops. When they clash, it triggers a storm of super hot, highly charged particles blasting out into space. It's called the solo wind. Astronauts in space can see it, but only when they close their eyes. Occasionally you see a little flash with your eyes shut. And that is an energetic particle coming through your head and interacting with the fluid inside your eye. And it makes a little light flash. And you see these every couple of minutes or so that you're awake with your eyes shut. If the astronauts were exposed to a lot more of the solo wind, it could kill them. raw_items.title: Non-compliance with a looming NERC deadline could cost clean energy owners and operators, big time
raw_items.summary: Owners of inverter-based resources (IBRs) have been required to register with NERC by May 15, 2026, or face steep penalties.

raw_items.title: How VPPs could improve grid reliability and save utilities 40% over gas peaker plants
raw_items.summary: A new report outlines how virtual power plants can free up capacity on the existing grid while highlighting how VPPs can also help meet new load growth driven by data centers.

raw_items.title: Are Trump’s efforts against clean energy in vain? – This Week in Cleantech
raw_items.summary: On this episode of the This Week in Cleantech podcast, Oliver Milman from The Guardian discusses how the clean energy industry is pushing forward despite Trump's aggressive efforts to suppress it.

raw_items.title: Iron flow and sodium-ion battery manufacturers to join forces
raw_items.summary: ESS Tech has signed a letter of intent with Alsym Energy to add sodium-ion batteries to its iron flow-focused portfolio.

raw_items.title: Hormuz Deadlock Persists as Trump Faces War-Powers Deadline at Home
raw_items.summary: Two months after the Iran war began, the Strait of Hormuz remains severely restricted for tanker traffic while U.S. President Donald Trump faces a key legal deadline at home under the 1973 War Powers Resolution. As of early Friday, May 1, the deadlock at the world’s most vital oil chokepoint, the Strait of Hormuz, doesn’t appear close to being resolved. The U.S. is doubling down on the naval blockade outside the Strait aimed at stopping Iranian oil exports. But Iran controls most vessel movement

raw_items.title: China Flips the Switch on Fuel Exports as Asia Runs Short
raw_items.summary: Beijing is reversing its curbs on refined fuel exports after halting shipments in the opening days of the U.S.-Iran conflict. This move suggests that Chinese domestic inventories are now at comfortable levels, allowing state refiners to reopen the export spigot, even as much of Asia remains gripped by a fuel shock caused by disrupted Gulf energy flows through the Hormuz chokepoint. There was chatter earlier this week that China's state-owned refiners were applying for government permits to resum
